word: G475 - ἀντιδιατίθεμαι - antidiatíthemai - an-tee-dee-at-eeth'-em-ahee - That oppose themselves

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀντιδιατίθεμαι

- Transliteration: Antidiatithemai

- Phonetic: an-tee-dee-at-eeth'-em-ahee

- Definition:

1. to place one's self in opposition, to oppose, to dispose in turn, to take in hand in turn, to retaliate

- Origin: from G473 and G1303

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G473 and G1303; to set oneself opposite that is be disputatious: - that oppose themselves.
